title: RepoPairCard Component
content:
The `RepoPairCard` component is responsible for displaying and managing a pair of repositories used for documentation synchronization within the DocMed application. It allows users to view the status of the repository pair, edit its name, and configure the target and documentation repositories. ## Key Exports - `RepoPairCard`: The main React component that renders the card for a repository pair. ## Components and Logic ### `StatusBadge` - Displays the current status of a repository pair (e.g., `ready`, `incomplete`, `not_configured`). - Uses different icons and styling based on the status. ### `EditableName` - Allows the user to edit the name of the repository pair. - Provides inline editing functionality with save and cancel options. - Persists changes by making a `PATCH` request to the `/api/repo-pairs/:pairId` endpoint. ### `ConnectionSlot` - Represents a single repository connection within the pair (either target or documentation repo). - Displays connection status, GitHub login, and the selected repository name. - Integrates with `RepoPicker` and `ExistingAccountPicker` for repository and account selection. - Shows required permissions for each repository type. ### `RepoPairCard` (Main Component) - Fetches `RepoPair` data and displays it in a card format. - Manages the deletion of a repository pair via a `DELETE` request to `/api/repo-pairs/:pairId`. - Renders two `ConnectionSlot` components for the target and documentation repositories. - Uses URL search parameters (`pick`, `pairId`) to automatically open the repository picker for a specific slot when navigating to the dashboard. ## Usage Example ```typescript import { RepoPairCard } from '@/app/dashboard/integrations/_components/RepoPairCard'; import type { RepoPair } from '@/lib/repo-pairs'; interface RepoPairCardProps { pair: RepoPair; } function MyComponent({ pair }: RepoPairCardProps) { return (
); } export default MyComponent; ``` This component is designed to be used within the dashboard section of the DocMed application to manage repository integrations.
